io.vertx.mutiny.core.http.HttpServer

Package
Description
io.vertx.mutiny.core
 
io.vertx.mutiny.core.http
 

  • Uses of HttpServer in io.vertx.mutiny.core

    Methods in io.vertx.mutiny.core that return HttpServer
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    HttpServer
    Vertx.createHttpServer()
    Create an HTTP/HTTPS server using default config.
    HttpServer
    Vertx.createHttpServer(io.vertx.core.http.HttpServerConfig config)
    Create an HTTP server using the specified config.
    HttpServer
    Vertx.createHttpServer(io.vertx.core.http.HttpServerConfig config, io.vertx.core.net.ServerSSLOptions sslOptions)
    Create an HTTP server using the specified config and the specified sslOptions
    HttpServer
    Vertx.createHttpServer(io.vertx.core.http.HttpServerOptions options)
    Create an HTTP/HTTPS server using the specified options
    HttpServer
    Vertx.createHttpServer(io.vertx.core.net.ServerSSLOptions sslOptions)
    Create an HTTP server using default config and the specified sslOptions.

  • Uses of HttpServer in io.vertx.mutiny.core.http

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final TypeArg<HttpServer>
    HttpServer.__TYPE_ARG
     
    Methods in io.vertx.mutiny.core.http that return HttpServer
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    HttpServer
    HttpServerBuilder.build()
    Build and return the server.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.closeAndForget()
    Close the server.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.connectionHandler(Consumer<HttpConnection> handler)
    Set a connection handler for the server.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.exceptionHandler(Consumer<Throwable> handler)
    Set an exception handler called for socket errors happening before the HTTP connection is established, e.g. during the TLS handshake.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.invalidRequestHandler(Consumer<HttpServerRequest> handler)
    Set a handler for handling invalid requests.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndAwait()
    Tell the server to start listening.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndAwait(int port)
    Like listen(int, String) but the server will listen on host "0.0.0.0" and port specified here ignoring any value in the io.vertx.mutiny.core.http.HttpServerOptions that was used when creating the server.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndAwait(int port, String host)
    Tell the server to start listening.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndAwait(io.vertx.core.net.SocketAddress address)
    Tell the server to start listening on the given address supplying a handler that will be called when the server is actually listening (or has failed).
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndForget()
    Tell the server to start listening.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndForget(int port)
    Like listen(int, String) but the server will listen on host "0.0.0.0" and port specified here ignoring any value in the io.vertx.mutiny.core.http.HttpServerOptions that was used when creating the server.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndForget(int port, String host)
    Tell the server to start listening.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.listenAndForget(io.vertx.core.net.SocketAddress address)
    Tell the server to start listening on the given address supplying a handler that will be called when the server is actually listening (or has failed).
    static HttpServer
    HttpServer.newInstance(io.vertx.core.http.HttpServer delegate)
    Creates a new instance of the HttpServer.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.requestHandler(Consumer<HttpServerRequest> handler)
    Set the request handler for the server to requestHandler.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.shutdownAndForget()
    Shutdown with a 30 seconds timeout (shutdown(Duration.ofSeconds(30))).
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.shutdownAndForget(long timeout, TimeUnit unit)
    Calls shutdown(Duration).
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.shutdownAndForget(Duration timeout)
    Initiate the server shutdown sequence.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.updateSSLOptionsAndForget(io.vertx.core.net.ServerSSLOptions options)
    Update the server with new SSL options, the update happens if the options object is valid and different from the existing options object.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.updateSSLOptionsAndForget(io.vertx.core.net.ServerSSLOptions options, boolean force)
    Update the server with new SSL options, the update happens if the options object is valid and different from the existing options object.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.updateTrafficShapingOptionsAndForget(io.vertx.core.net.TrafficShapingOptions options)
    Update the server with new traffic options, the update happens if the options object is valid and different from the existing options object.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.webSocketHandler(Consumer<ServerWebSocket> handler)
    Set the WebSocket handler for the server to wsHandler.
    HttpServer
    HttpServer.webSocketHandshakeHandler(Consumer<ServerWebSocketHandshake> handler)
    Set a handler for WebSocket handshake.
    Methods in io.vertx.mutiny.core.http that return types with arguments of type HttpServer
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    io.smallrye.mutiny.Uni<HttpServer>
    HttpServer.listen()
    Tell the server to start listening.
    io.smallrye.mutiny.Uni<HttpServer>
    HttpServer.listen(int port)
    Like listen(int, String) but the server will listen on host "0.0.0.0" and port specified here ignoring any value in the io.vertx.mutiny.core.http.HttpServerOptions that was used when creating the server.
    io.smallrye.mutiny.Uni<HttpServer>
    HttpServer.listen(int port, String host)
    Tell the server to start listening.
    io.smallrye.mutiny.Uni<HttpServer>
    HttpServer.listen(io.vertx.core.net.SocketAddress address)
    Tell the server to start listening on the given address supplying a handler that will be called when the server is actually listening (or has failed).
